sections.paragraph: an unused root, which had, I expect, the signification of treading, trampling; like the Gr. στείβω; hence נָתִיב a beaten path. As to the primary syllables tab, tap, and pat, imitating the sound of treading; see above. בּוּס p. 108 , and דָּבַב, טָפַף. Arab. نتب is, to be lofty, to be swollen up; whence I have elsewhere supposed נָתִיב to be derived; i.e. a raised and fortified way; like מְסִלָּה from סָלַל; but נ׳ never denotes a public and royal road, such as was raised up and formed by art, but always a footpath.
